rationale: At 50165d81, grounding supports Option B. bead.WorktreeList invokes bd worktree list --json; approve already imports internal/bead; and FinalizeEpic obtains candidates through WorktreeOps.List, filters bead/ branches, ancestry-checks, then merges. BranchExists remains the unchanged rev-parse boolean probe. The proposed added gate is epic-scoped, fail-closed for worktree-list/ancestry errors, additive to the shared predicate, and uses the same enumeration source as the merge loop, closing the identified transient probe-miss race under the stated serialization model. The narrower two-List concurrent-creation window is explicitly disclosed, Fact 2 is correctly limited to persistent ref failure, and AC13 directly falsifies probe-only regression. AC10 replaces the already-green ownership grep with a genuinely absent named structural test. Both new tests are absent at the pinned SHA; BranchExistsE, ScanOrphanedClosedBeads, and show-ref Go usage remain absent. No internal/bead change lands. Commit e239c4e5 itself changes only spec.md (16/15); the wider 8b07e89f..e239c4e5 range additionally contains decision-review artifacts, which is non-functional and does not affect the spec claims.

findings.detail: Grounded in the same bd worktree enumeration source used by FinalizeEpic; additive, epic-scoped, and fail-closed on list or ancestry errors.
The named test is absent at 50165d81 and its probe-miss/live-worktree, error, and no-false-positive cases are discriminating.

The transient race is closed by same-source enumeration; the remaining two-call concurrent-creation window and serialization assumption are explicitly bounded, with Fact 2 limited to persistent failure.
The absent named structural test is RED at the pinned SHA and directly enforces exactly one workflow claimant.
